transcript: Hello everyone. I am Dr. Prakash, Co-founder of DeCharge. We are building the Helium for EV charging. So, yeah, I think just to simplify for better understanding, decentralized EV charging infra around the world. But why is it necessary? We're looking into problem statement of lack of EV charging infra for a lot of EVs across the world. And what does this actually mean? Imagine you are waiting to charge your car and there are like 100 other vehicles waiting to charge. So that's not an ideal situation. This is exactly why EVs are not scaling even though there's like a large global mandate. And this is like a $500 billion market failure. And how did we solve the solution? Like, we took the traditional DePIN model and stretched it as much as possible. Let's say even without the incentives. There was still a lot of interest in terms of a lot of decentralized community funding that helped us scale to a lot of charging stations. And, but we were not satisfied with it. We took it even further. How do we make it scalable, right? The fast charging market is like massive because everyone wants to get their cars charged and get onto the business. So that's how we used like the DePIN-Fi approach where there's like a massive demand for EV charging across the world. And these give like crazy yields alongside token incentives as well. So that was an approach we took, like close to like 500... 500k worth of charging infra that was actually like tokenized for people to invest as well. And in terms of traction, we've grown crazily across Asia specifically, and around like 100 devices plus in the US. And with crazy community partners like Tesla Owners Silicon Valley. This kind of proved that... this is a product that we can actually change... address to the average user who just doesn't want to deal with anything complex on the frontend. We have like crazy... we've had like really good enterprise partners. Of course, with the whole tariff situation, it was difficult to export, but we made sure that we partnered with like real... a big publicly listed company. It was Wallbox. This meant that they have like around close to like 1 million charging... charging nodes across the world. And they... we recently did a deal with them which is like a big news in the clean energy tech. So close to like 1 million chargers onboarded onto our network. And we have like a great team to execute this to scale even further. My co-founder has had three exits in a very similar problem statement in Asia. And we have like a team that has the ability to scale across US and in Asia as well. That's it. If you like to check out, check out our socials. Thank you.

Battery storage solutions
# Delta Charge secures 4.3 million US dollars
Munich-based climate tech start-up accelerates electrification of logistics with battery storage and integrated software. Investors from energy, mobility and industry focus on depot-based charging infrastructure.
News by[![Marc Nemitz](https://cdn.startbase.com/images/author/m-nemitz/?webp=1&w=24)Marc Nemitz](https://www.startbase.com/author/m-nemitz/)·München, 20. November 2025
![Delta Charge secures 4.3 million US dollars](https://cdn.startbase.com/images/magazine/2025/11/Delta-Charge.-Left-to-right_-Connor-Hanafee-Filip-Hes-Johannes-Kirnberger.jpg?webp=1&w=1024)Delta Charge Connor Hanafee, Filip Hes, Johannes Kirnberger | (c) Delta Charge
The German-Swedish start-up Delta Charge, which specializes in charging and battery storage solutions for electric commercial and heavy-duty vehicles, has closed an oversubscribed financing round of USD 4.3 million. The round was led by investors[Vireo Ventures](https://www.startbase.com/organization/vireo-ventures/)and Rethink Ventures. In addition, high-ranking executives from Audi and Allianz, German[family offices](https://www.startbase.com/knowhow/family-office/)and several founders from the Munich climate tech ecosystem also participated.
The funds are intended to accelerate the expansion of a pan-European network for depot charging infrastructure and industrial battery storage.
### Combination of battery storage, charging infrastructure and energy management
Delta Charge was founded in Munich in 2024 and addresses a key bottleneck in the electrification of heavy-duty transportation: the availability of reliable, scalable depot charging solutions. The company combines battery storage systems (BESS), charging infrastructure, energy management and transparent fixed-price models into an integrated solution.
With the support of its Scandinavian partner Delta Capacity, an experienced developer and investor in large-scale battery storage systems, Delta Charge plans to invest more than 300 million euros by 2030. The aim is to build a Europe-wide energy system that provides 1.8 terawatt hours of renewable energy per year.
### Rising energy demand due to electrified truck fleets
The financing round comes at a time when the electrification of commercial and heavy-duty vehicles is gaining momentum. The power requirements of these vehicles are growing rapidly and could reach the level of European data centers within a few years.
At the same time, the transportation sector is currently responsible for almost half of Europe's CO₂ emissions. Electrification offers great potential for reducing emissions and lowering operating costs. However, efficient charging and storage solutions in logistics depots are required for the economical operation of electric trucks.
### Investors emphasize the importance of integrated infrastructure
Investors see Delta Charge as an important component of the energy and mobility transition.
> > In future, the economic operation of electric truck fleets will depend on intelligent depot charging solutions that combine battery storage, charging infrastructure and integrated software.
> > > Jens Philipp Klein, General Partner at Rethink Ventures
The Delta Charge team has experience in setting up large energy infrastructure projects as wel

# 10 Automotive Start-Ups graduate Accelerator Programme
[Back toPosts](https://www.apcuk.co.uk/news-events/news/)
**27 November 202****5**
10 early-stage start-ups and SMEs have graduated from the 8thand final wave of the government’s award-winning Technology Developer Accelerator Programme (TDAP), facilitated by the Advanced Propulsion Centre UK (APC).
Marking their successful completion of the funding and business support programme, the 10 groundbreaking clean mobility businesses presented their innovations to investors, industry experts, and government officials on 26 November 2025 at 30 Euston Square, London.
The event also featured a fireside chat titled ‘Start-ups need ambition to win: Does the UK foster that confidence?’ featuring Ian Constance, CEO, Advanced Propulsion Centre UK; Tim Woolmer, CTO, YASA Motors; Kieran Borrett, Director, Plug and Play UK, and Andrew Whitehead, CEO, Protean Electric.
Two start-ups were recognised for‘Best Pitch’ – Enough Energy – voted by the audience and for ‘Most Promising Start-Up’ – Polaron – voted by a panel of industry judges including Marcus Henry, JLR, Surakat Kudehinbu, Green Angel Syndicate, Matt Hicks, Fortescue Investment Management, Kieran Borrett, Plug and Play UK, Ian Constance, APC UK, Julian Hetherington APC UK, and Dave Matthews, APC.
![](https://www.apcuk.co.uk/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/TDAP-Wave-8-Demo-Day-1024x768-1-300x204.jpg)
**Rik Adams, Innovation Delivery Director, APC**said:
> *> “> These 10 **> impressive businesses have worked for 18 months, developing their technology concepts, but more importantly, defining their commercial position through our targeted mentoring. They are now ready to engage the market and gain tractio

# ![Screenshot 2025-10-27 120234.png](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/20f01498-1a8f-42d5-83e0-c1ef8b11789f?size=3)
# Anome Protocol Unveils Super Ecosystem: Integrating GameFi, NFTFi, and DePIN for Mass Web3 Adoption
November 28, 2025 01:10 ET| Source:[ANOME](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/ANOME)FollowANOME
Share
* * * * * * * * SINGAPORE, Nov. 28, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Anome Protocol, the premier modular Web3 operating system, today unveils its expanded super ecosystem, seamlessly merging GameFi, NFTFi, DeFi, and DePIN under a unified framework. Designed to onboard 1 million new Web2 users by 2026, this initiative leverages high-frequency behavior mechanics and gamified assets to democratize Web3 participation, with strategic support from partners like BTG (Blockchain Technology Group) and CyberCharge.
![Anome Protocol](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/33eaae18-f5e6-4aa3-836f-e2b6f0eefb21/anome-protocol.jpg)[](#)
[Anome](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=j5qzi3t5jBY00qlZqNfbXh38aAR93FdWdPUHTy_qrzHKSqw8ypqZQBsSsV3Ia8_fp0eDgYs8qzsvFagZpwYbRA==)'s breakthrough comes on the heels of its successful $BNOME to $ANOME token migration in early November 2025. The $ANOME token now trades on[KuCoin](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=Ldq8Y1apOZoBpX_yPGboZwrCSP4C4DugYrK-6n-tsqTVkQTSLLTjO7jsWM4vDWW9MsOccx3sGmGu6mfe41p7h-HZCqBrdnYm9ocfQwtB-UQ=)and Binance Alpha, achieving 24-hour volume exceeding $6 million and a stable market cap surpassing $10 million. This growth underscores Anome's core mission: fostering a "Growth Loop" for sustainable economic activity. By integrating with select partners, Anome gains enhanced infrastructure and global reach, including access to a $5 million co-investment pool from BTG's Singapore Web3 Incubation Fund for key initiatives like AnoMEME, the ERC-404 meme token launchpad.
Anome's ecosystem is built around three foundational pillars: Game (P2E mini-games enhanced by NFT staking), Asset (NFTFi tools that unlock liquidity without forcing users to liquidate holdings), and Growth (innovative token lock-and-release mechanisms to ensure long-term viability). Users can stake $ANOME to mint exclusive NFTs, dive into profit-sharing gameplay, or borrow stablecoins collateralized by their NFT portfolios—all powered by a multi-chain architecture spanning Ethereum and Arbitrum for effortless interoperability.
A key highlight is Anome's integration with DePIN via CyberCharge's Charge-to-Earn model. Real-world users earn $CBS tokens through everyday actions like phone charging, which can then be swapped into $ANOME for seamless entry into the ecosystem. This creates a closed economic loop, bridging physical behaviors with digital rewards and amplifying user retention through endogenous incentives.
![Anome Protocol 2](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/06b39198-6956-478f-a8d7-deaabfef5759/anome-protocol-2.jpg)[](#)
Deloitte's latest report projects the DeFi-NFT hybrid market to hit $100 billion by 2027, and Anome is positioned as a frontrunner by gamifying real-world and virtual assets alike. "We're transforming Web3 into an accessible game where every participant thrives," shares AL, CTO of Anome. "Our Growth Loop isn't just theoretical—it's engineered for compounded ROI, with features like AnoMEME enabling meme tokens to stake directly into DePIN nodes for yields up to 200% quarterly. Partnerships provide the scaling boost, but Anome's modular design is the true engine dri

tags.impact_reason: DeCharge's demo day pitch shows potential in the EV charging space within the DePIN sector, however, it's still early and impact is speculative.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the EV Charging Plug and Cable as the semantic anchor because it's the tangible, physical object at the heart of this DePIN story. The visual of its cable transforming into a network of glowing, data-filled strands directly represents the 'tokenization' of physical infrastructure. For the homepage, I integrated the diegetic text '1M' as a holographic projection from the charging port. This number is the core metric from the headline ('1 Million EV Chargers'), signifying the massive scale of the network being unlocked.
visual_idea.description: A macro, detailed shot focusing on the point of transformation. We see the textured, rubber-like sheath of a conventional EV power cable dramatically breaking apart, revealing a brilliant, dense core of pulsing, thread-like fiber optics. The lighting is dramatic, catching the intricate textures of the old material and the intense, energetic glow of the new decentralized network emerging from within.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, detailed shot focusing on the precise point of transformation where a conventional EV power cable dramatically breaks apart. The textured, rubber-like sheath, rendered with intricate micro-textural details and realistic subsurface scattering, is seen fracturing and peeling back. This dramatic rupture reveals a brilliant, dense core of pulsing, thread-like fiber optics. The fibers emanate an intense, energetic glow, suggesting a complex, interconnected decentralized network emerging from within, with light passing through the translucent polycarbonate-like sheath fragments. Volumetric lighting casts dramatic, high-contrast beams, sharply highlighting the rough textures of the old material and intensely illuminating the vibrant, neon-like glow of the new fiber optic core. The composition is a perfectly square 1:1 aspect ratio, with strong symmetry and a central focus on the transformation, filling the entire frame with rich textures and dynamic interplay of light and shadow. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic quality.

editorial.headline: DeCharge Partners with Wallbox to Tokenize 1 Million EV Chargers
editorial.standfirst: Solana-based DeCharge announces a strategic integration with NYSE-listed Wallbox, unlocking access to a global network of over 1 million existing charging points. This "brownfield" strategy bypasses traditional hardware bottlenecks, enabling immediate scaling of decentralized energy infrastructure.
key_takeaways: Strategic partnership with **Wallbox** (NYSE: WBX) to integrate ~1 million global charging nodes.
Backed by **Lemniscap** and **Colosseum** following a $2.5M Seed round.
**DePIN-Fi** model has already tokenized $500k of charging infrastructure assets.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
DeCharge has flipped the DePIN playbook. While most decentralized infrastructure projects struggle with the "cold start" problem of deploying hardware, Co-founder **Dr. Prakash Kamaraj** revealed a strategic integration with **Wallbox** (NYSE: WBX). The deal effectively turns Wallbox’s installed base of over 1 million chargers into potential nodes on the DeCharge network. This "brownfield" approach—layering Web3 incentives on top of existing Web2 hardware—positions DeCharge to scale faster than any competitor attempting to build a network from scratch.

The project introduces "DePIN-Fi," a hybrid model merging physical infrastructure with DeFi yields. Instead of relying solely on utility rewards, DeCharge allows for the **tokenization of charging assets**, enabling investors to fund specific high-yield chargers. The platform has already tokenized approximately $500k worth of infrastructure, creating a liquid market for EV charging revenue. This financial layer solves the capex-heavy nature of energy grid expansion by allowing retail and institutional liquidity to flow directly into hardware deployment.

The macro thesis is driven by a $500 billion market failure: global EV mandates are outpacing infrastructure development, creating massive bottlenecks. DeCharge’s solution is built on **Solana** to handle the high-frequency transactions required for real-time energy settlements. With a leadership team boasting three previous exits in the mobility sector and backing from **Lemniscap** and **Colosseum**, the project is moving beyond the "science experiment" phase of DePIN into institutional-grade execution.
